Wade has called into a rest stop and decides to get food for the road. He buys a sandwich to eat now, one for the road, and one for in the evening. He also buys 2 drinks. If the drinks cost $4 each and Wade spends a total of $26 then how much, in dollars, did the sandwiches each cost? The total cost of the drinks is [number] because [calculation]. Wade spent [total] on sandwiches and drinks combined, which means [remaining amount] was spent on sandwiches. Since there are [number of sandwiches], each sandwich cost [cost per sandwich].

The lab [lab name] is where [lab activity] takes place, and [lab equipment] is used to [lab purpose]. Wade’s total expenditure is [total amount], which includes [number of drinks] drinks at [cost per drink] each. Subtracting the cost of the drinks from the total gives [remaining amount], which is divided by [number of sandwiches] to find the cost per sandwich. This calculation ensures [correct answer] is determined accurately.
